worked_example_ref.problem_statement_digest: windowed(seq, n) with n=0 silently yielded a single empty tuple () instead of rejecting the invalid size; n must be > 0. The guard let an out-of-contract size through as a degenerate result. Make windowed raise ValueError for n<=0.

lesson_body: A size or count parameter with a documented lower bound must be validated as a strict inequality at the entry point; treating a zero value as a degenerate-but-valid case silently violates the contract instead of surfacing it as an error. Reject the entire out-of-contract range eagerly so callers receive a clear signal rather than a nonsensical result.
